title: The finite weighted P0 average is additive

source.citation: S. C. Brenner and L. R. Scott, The Mathematical Theory of Finite Element Methods, 3rd ed., Springer, 2008, Ch. 4.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The finite weighted P0 average is additive in the cell-value function.
assumptions: u and v are cell value functions
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted average of a pointwise sum equals the sum of the weighted averages.

declarations.name: weightedP0Average
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Average {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) : Real := (∑ i ∈ s, m i * u i) / (∑ i ∈ s, m i)

declarations.name: weightedP0Residual
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Residual {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - weightedP0Average s m u

declarations.name: weightedP0Average_add
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Average_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u v : ι → Real) : weightedP0Average s m (fun i => u i + v i) = weightedP0Average s m u + weightedP0Average s m v

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index weighted P0 projection fact `weightedP0Average_add` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedP0Average_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u v : ι → Real) : weightedP0Average s m (fun i => u i + v i) = weightedP0Average s m u + weightedP0Average s m v
Use pointwise distributivity, Finset.sum_add_distrib, and ring normalization.

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or weighted-projection lemma needed for the proof.
